notable_excerpts: A key contributor to revenue mix improvement was the electric vehicle portfolio, which continued to scale rapidly during the quarter, growing nearly 40% over the previous one.
Our e-autos recorded their highest-ever levels during the quarter, both in billing and retail terms, and we exited the quarter in December back to the number 1 position in the segment.

We now possess the widest e-auto and e-cargo portfolio, spanning different vehicle sizes and battery capacities ranging from 9-kilowatt hour to 18-kilowatt hour, which is the biggest battery in the business.
Our performance improved sharply during the quarter as we came out of supply chain disruptions, enabling a rapid scale-up.
We regained nearly 500 basis points of market share during the quarter and made a return back to the leadership cluster.
The planned governance and structural changes to the Supervisory Board have since been implemented.
The Supervisory and Management Boards have been reconstituted to align with the revised ownership structure and the governance framework.

chain_of_thought: The earnings call highlighted significant progress in the environmental pillar with a strong focus on electric vehicles and battery capacities. Social aspects were less detailed, focusing on supply chain improvements. Governance disclosures were positive, with board restructuring and risk management being key themes.
strengths: Strong focus on electric vehicle expansion
